steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Research notes — the goal is to use a quotation to emphasize a potential problem with the wood-wide web theory. The only critical quotation in the notes is Karst and colleagues' concern that recent CMN claims are "disconnected from evidence."

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: To accomplish the goal, the choice must (a) quote and (b) point to a problem. Only choice C does both, using "disconnected from evidence" to highlight the gap between popular claims and supporting evidence.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Describing trees as "super-cooperators," Simard first suggested...': the quoted phrase is positive and supports the theory rather than emphasizing a problem. ✗
  • Choice B — '...Karst and colleagues note that common mycorrhizal networks "have captured the interest of broad audiences."': quotes neutral language about popularity, not the critical concern; does not emphasize a problem. ✗
  • Choice C — '...Karst and colleagues expressed concern that recent claims about common mycorrhizal networks are "disconnected from evidence."': uses the critical quotation to highlight the problem — that claims outrun the evidence. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Despite the concerns expressed in the 2022 study... the wood-wide web theory remains popular.': contains no quotation and therefore cannot accomplish the stated goal. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
